The best way to predict the future is to create it. — Peter Drucker
First, solve the problem. Then, write the code. — John Johnson
Talk is cheap. Show me the code. — Linus Torvalds
Programs must be written for people to read, and only incidentally for machines to execute. — Harold Abelson
Any fool can write code that a computer can understand. Good programmers write code that humans can understand. — Martin Fowler
The only way to go fast is to go well. — Robert C. Martin
Simplicity is the soul of efficiency. — Austin Freeman
Make it work, make it right, make it fast. — Kent Beck
The most damaging phrase in the language is: 'We've always done it this way.' — Grace Hopper
It's not a bug — it's an undocumented feature. — Anonymous
One man's crappy software is another man's full-time job. — Jessica Gaston
The best error message is the one that never shows up. — Thomas Fuchs
Java is to JavaScript as Car is to Carpet. — Chris Heilmann
A commit a day keeps the bugs away. — Unknown Developer
Code is like humor. When you have to explain it, it's bad. — Cory House
Fix the cause, not the symptom. — Steve Maguire
Optimism is an occupational hazard of programming. — Kent Beck
The function of good software is to make the complex appear simple. — Grady Booch
Before software can be immortal, it has to be usable. — Mark Minasi
Measuring programming progress by lines of code is like measuring aircraft building progress by weight. — Bill Gates
Software engineering is a discipline whose time has come. — Margaret Hamilton
The most dangerous phrase is 'it works on my machine'. — Anonymous
Always code as if the person who ends up maintaining your code is a violent psychopath who knows where you live. — John Woods
Deleted code is debugged code. — Jeff Sickel
If debugging is the process of removing bugs, then programming must be the process of putting them in. — Edsger Dijkstra
Computers are fast; programmers keep them slow. — Anonymous
It works on my machine — famous last words. — Every Developer
A user interface is like a joke. If you have to explain it, it's not that good. — Martin LeBlanc
The best programs are the ones written when the programmer is supposed to be working on something else. — Melinda Varian
Every great developer you know got there by solving problems they were unqualified to solve until they actually did it. — Patrick McKenzie
Code never lies, comments sometimes do. — Ron Jeffries
Debugging is twice as hard as writing the code in the first place. — Brian Kernighan
The only valid measurement of code quality: WTFs per minute. — Anonymous
Software is a great combination of art and engineering. — Bill Gates
Don't comment bad code — rewrite it. — Brian Kernighan
There are two ways to write error-free programs; only the third one works. — Alan Perlis
Walking on water and developing software from a specification are easy if both are frozen. — Edward Berard
If you automate a disaster, you get an automated disaster. — Anonymous
The most important property of a program is whether it accomplishes the intention of its user. — C.A.R. Hoare
Refactoring is not a luxury, it's a necessity. — Martin Fowler
Software is a gas: it expands to fill its container. — Nathan Myhrvold
The best time to start was yesterday. The next best time is now. — Unknown
Small steps, every day. That's how mountains are climbed. — Anonymous
Building software is like building a house — you need a solid foundation. — Unknown
You don't have to be great to start, but you have to start to be great. — Zig Ziglar
The expert in anything was once a beginner. — Helen Hayes
Consistency over intensity. — James Clear
The habit of improvement is the key to long-term success. — Unknown
One percent better every day. That's the goal. — Anonymous
Building in public builds better products. — Unknown Developer
